message:
fix(tests): unblock full-review suite — btrfs CRC, asupersync 0.3 headroom, clippy too_many_lines

Three independently-reproducing test failures surfaced in the 2026-04-22
full-review session, all landed together because they each gate a
different leaf of `cargo test --all-features`:

1. ffs-core cross_crate_integration (bd-5o3i6 sibling for cross-crate
   path): `build_btrfs_fsops_image` now stamps CRC32C over
   [0x20..nodesize] on both the root_leaf and fs_leaf tree blocks using
   ffs_types::crc32c, writing the checksum into the leading 4 bytes.
   Two previously-failing cross-crate btrfs tests (root/fs leaf walks)
   stop panicking with checksum-mismatch now that the fixture matches
   the on-disk invariant already enforced by build_btrfs_readdir_image.

2. ffs-fuse degraded_pressure tests: Warning-level headroom is raised
   0.35 → 0.75 and Critical-level headroom 0.08 → 0.15 so the asserted
   no-impact / heavy-impact boundaries line up with asupersync 0.3's
   light/level-3 thresholds (documented in-line). The FSM itself is
   unchanged — only the test inputs are adjusted for the new scale.

3. ffs-harness fuse_e2e::create_btrfs_image_with_subvolumes carries a
   `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` now that clippy flags it on the
   full-feature workspace scan. The helper genuinely needs its length to
   drive `sudo mkfs.btrfs` through the subvolume / snapshot / send-stream
   fixtures in one place; splitting it would fragment the sudo guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
